questions.prompt:
The graph of f  prime,  the derivative of a function f, is shown above. Which of the following could be the  
graph of f ? 
I. The graph consists of two line segments.   The first segment begins at an open dot on the positive y axis and moves down, crosses the x axis at x equals 2, and ends at an open dot below x equals 4.  The second segment begins at an open dot above the end of the first segment, but still below x equals 4, and moves horizontally to an open dot below x equals 8.

II. The graph consists of two curves.  The first curve begins at an open dot on the positive y axis. The curve moves down to a low point above x equals 1, moves up to a high point above x equals 3, and moves down to end at an open dot above x equals 4.  The second curve begins at an open dot below the end of the first curve but still above x equals 4, moves up to a high point above x equals 6, and curves down to end at an open dot above x equals 8 that is level with the first open dot on the second curve.

III. The graph consists of a curve.  The curve begins at an open dot on the positive y axis. The curve moves down to a low point above x equals 1, moves up to a high point above x equals 3, and moves down to a sharp turn above x equals 4.  After the sharp turn, the curve continues up to a high point above x equals 6, and curves down to end at an open dot above x equals 8 that is level with the sharp turn.
